title: lippia antaica
intro: Lippia antaica, also known as Chilean verbena, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and colds, and soothing digestive discomfort. Lippia antaica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall well-being.
